name: TensorFlow Lite Python (Edge ML Inference)

performance.notes: Highly optimized for ARM/x86 edge; hardware delegate for Neural Engine/GPU

agent_readiness.notes: TFLite runtime for edge/embedded ML inference. import tflite_runtime.interpreter as tflite. interpreter = tflite.Interpreter(model_path='model.tflite'). interpreter.allocate_tensors(). input_details = interpreter.get_input_details(). output_details = interpreter.get_output_details(). interpreter.set_tensor(input_details[0]['index'], input_data). interpreter.invoke(). output = interpreter.get_tensor(output_details[0]['index']). Hardware delegates: tflite.load_delegate('libedgetpu.so.1'). TFLite model from: tf.lite.TFLiteConverter.from_keras_model(model). Quantization: post-training quantization (INT8, FP16). Models: TFLite model zoo (MobileNet, EfficientDet, etc.).

gotchas: Must explicitly allocate_tensors() before first inference
Input/output tensor shapes are fixed at compile time — dynamic shapes need SignatureDef
Quantization may reduce accuracy — test post-quantization accuracy
Hardware delegate (Edge TPU, GPU) requires specific driver installation
Model conversion from SavedModel/Keras can fail for complex ops
best_when: Edge inference on Raspberry Pi, Coral TPU, mobile, or microcontrollers
When full TensorFlow is too heavy and ONNX Runtime is unavailable
Google Coral Edge TPU inference with Edge TPU delegate
Low-latency on-device ML for computer vision (MobileNet, EfficientDet)
avoid_when: Server-side inference (use ONNX Runtime or TensorFlow Serving)
When PyTorch is the training framework (use ONNX or ExecuTorch)
Complex dynamic models with unsupported ops
summary: TFLite Python runtime runs TFLite models on edge devices. Interpreter('model.tflite'). allocate_tensors(). set_tensor() → invoke() → get_tensor(). Hardware delegates: Edge TPU, GPU. INT8/FP16 quantization. Apache-2.0 by Google. Best for Raspberry Pi, Coral TPU, and mobile edge inference. Must allocate_tensors() first. Fixed tensor shapes. Quantization may reduce accuracy. For servers prefer ONNX Runtime.
